what_it_does: This file is a design brief / front-end handoff for building a cognition authoring UI inside Claude Design; it states what the screen is, what backend endpoints it calls, the data shapes, the UX flow, the design-system constraints, hard governance laws, and unresolved open questions.
observations: It is explicitly written for Anthropic's Claude Design tool (human clicks + describes, tool reads/writes repo and renders live).
The backend is described as built, tested, and live, while no front-end for authoring exists yet.
It proposes one Authoring region (a sibling of the existing read-only CognitionView) with three panels: role authoring, rule authoring, and preview-a-turn.
It lists exact backend endpoints, request/response shapes, and data shapes such as RoleFieldSpec, RuleAST, RuleDecl, rule_ops, and destination_kinds.
Every authoring write must surface a proposal; the operator approves via /api/resolve and it is applied via /api/apply — never auto-applied.
It enumerates nine hard laws that the UI must obey, including REFLECTS-NEVER-OWNS, PROPOSE-NOT-APPLY, closed grammar, and operator-only face.
It grounds visual design in the existing token system (gold primary, Fraunces + IBM Plex Mono) and mandates reuse of kit.tsx, NodeConfigForm, CognitionView, Inbox, and ProposeAffordance.
It includes open questions for Tim about approval gestures, region layout, lane destination UX, directory-scan source scope, and persona theming.
It offers three next-step options: deep rule-builder trace, wireframe sketch, or tentative first render of the role palette.
standouts: The propose-not-apply governance loop is the central hard constraint.
The rule-builder is the net-new centerpiece: a recursive composer over a closed 16-op grammar with depth-capping and renderable checks.
All dropdowns must be populated from /api/cognition_info at runtime; the FE must never hardcode ops, destinations, field types, or models.
The brief insists on reusing existing components and patterns rather than creating bespoke ones.
Several UX/scope decisions are deliberately left open for human steering.
conventions: Uses file:line citations to ground claims in the repo.
Follows the existing registry-renderer pattern where backend changes appear live without FE edits.
Follows the existing design-system tokens and component vocabulary.
Breaks away from direct-write authoring tools by requiring surfaced operator approval.
Structures content as plain-terms overview, exact contract, UX flow, design bar, laws, and open questions.
concerns: Multiple open questions mean the brief is not fully actionable without Tim's decisions.
The lane destination and directory-scan source node are acknowledged as under-specified or unbuilt.
The UI it describes does not yet exist, so the brief assumes Claude Design can support the proposed interaction model.
Endpoint shapes are described as verified-live examples, but as a design brief the file itself is not executable and could drift from the backend.
notes: A governance-heavy design brief for the cognition authoring surface that insists every edit must be proposed, reviewed, and approved before it goes live.
questions: Which open questions will be decided before implementation begins?
How will the recursive rule-builder remain operable on mobile touchscreens?
What behavior is expected if /api/cognition_info changes while an authoring form is open?
Will the UI combine approve and apply into one gesture or keep them as two explicit steps?
How should the under-specified lane destination be presented to operators?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — it presents itself as a Claude-Design-ready brief and delivers a detailed specification for the proposed authoring UI.
apparent_themes: Operator governance and deliberation before live changes
Registry-driven, generic rendering instead of hardcoded front-end lists
Reuse of existing patterns and components
intention_signals: To give Claude Design a complete, verifiable, law-bound brief so it can build the right front-end without inventing backend behavior, while preserving the existing governance model and aesthetic.
novelty: It is a design brief that embeds hard backend governance laws as non-negotiable constraints and explicitly leaves UX/scope decisions as open questions for a human operator.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, citation-backed specification for a proposed cognition authoring UI, including the endpoint contract, data shapes, governance approval loop, design-system constraints, and unresolved decisions, which a fused system would keep as the source-of-intent for the front-end build.
summary_for_embedding: A Claude-Design brief specifying the proposed operator-facing cognition authoring UI for roles and rules, including backend endpoint contract, governance approval loop, design-system constraints, and open questions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide Claude Design with enough grounded detail to render the cognition authoring surface while respecting existing backend contracts and governance laws.
